summary.intent: Add a TagsInput component implementation and get it through the CI pre-push gate with sufficient test coverage.
summary.outcome: All 10 CI gates passed with ars-components branch coverage at ≥90%, commit amended with branch-coverage tests, and branch pushed ready for PR.

repo: The project uses `cargo xci-fast` as a pre-push gate that enforces a 90% branch coverage threshold for ars-components.
The coverage gate uses lcov data; test-code `assert!` panic arms count as uncovered branches but are considered inherent/acceptable.
The gate runs 10 discrete checks; coverage is one of them.

code.finding: Short-circuit or conditional branch arm that required explicit test coverage for out-of-range part indices.
Readonly-not-disabled path and a companion branch; both needed targeted tests to push coverage over 90%.
Non-editable Enter-on-tag branch arm required a dedicated test case.
allow_duplicates commit/paste and empty-delimiter InputChange branches; line 1009 was the last uncovered production branch after the first coverage pass.
Blur-add edge-case branches in this range needed coverage; lines 1165 and 1192 were specifically identified.
Additional conditional branch arms in this range were uncovered and addressed in the targeted test pass.

workflow: Running the coverage gate first revealed a 0.1% shortfall (89.9%), requiring a targeted second pass of branch-coverage tests before pushing.
Parsing lcov branch data directly (BRH lines) is an efficient way to identify exactly which line numbers lack coverage without re-running the full suite.
Amending the commit after confirming the gate green keeps the branch-coverage tests bundled with the implementation in a single commit.

friction: Initial gate run showed 89.9% branch coverage — just 0.1% under the 90% threshold — requiring an additional round of targeted tests before the push could proceed.
Test-code `assert!` panic arms inflate the uncovered-branch count in lcov, requiring manual filtering to identify real production gaps.
open_items: Branch coverage is exactly at the 90% floor after the fix; any future changes to tags_input that add uncovered branches will immediately trip the gate — consider adding a few more tests as a buffer.
PR was not yet opened in the transcript; that step was queued but not confirmed complete.
